podcasts.name: Davie Fogarty
podcasts.title: The story behind a billion dollar brand
podcasts.transcript: And you just recently sold Kind Bar. How did that feel? And can you run me through how much it was as well? >> It was a lot. >> It starts with a B, doesn't it? >> It starts with many B's. >> Many B's. >> But I didn't do it for the money, right? Like it's not I did care about the money. I'm an passionate enlightened capitalist. I really do believe that market forces can help us create value. But there was a bigger purpose, right? I wanted to honor my father. You know, the reason we called it kind is because of my dad. because he was in the Holocaust. He was in a concentration camp and in spite of all the darkness that he experienced as a 12 to 15 year old when he was liberated from a concentration camp, he was 15 and 1/2 years old. He was close to 6 feet tall and he weighed 70 lb. Wow. >> So he was almost less than human and yet he always remembered that he survived because of the kindness of others. He always remembered that it was thanks to other people's kindness that he survived. So we named the company Kind after him. He passed away that year. The year that we conceived kind. It's such a dark year for me and in some ways also one of the brightest years. It's so weird to explain that. >> Yeah. >> I almost didn't launch kind. I lost my dad. I grew so much internally by meditating and thinking about my father but thinking what what example would he want me to do for the world. And all of a sudden things started coming together. I can't even explain it. And then you became a multi-billion dollar company within years. And to your question about how does it feel to sell your baby, it's very hard because Kind was not just a business enterprise. It's a mission to bring kindness to the world, to do the kind thing to for others, one snack and one act at a
